title: Technology
content:
The supply chain risk designation of the artificial intelligence firm is a first for a US company.

There's a free automated tool that removes information about you from Google search results. For some reason, most people don't use it.

The advice you get about talking to chatbots can be truly bizarre, and totally useless. Here's what actually works.

New technologies tested at the Deaflympics in Tokyo are creating new ways of experience the atmosphere at sporting events.

Rae began speaking to Barry after a difficult divorce, but Barry lives on an old model of ChatGPT that's being shut down.

A gold medallist has developed a vibrating starting block to give deaf athletes clearer, fairer race starts.

A Cardiff University researcher recreated her own voice using a deathbot - but found it strange.

Technology to get us around was a big theme at CES 2026. Tech Now tests out some of the latest innovations.

Tech Now experiences Lego's new Smart Brick, designed to bring physical play to next level.

A project to introduce unmanned sites is part of a broader adoption of digital technologies.

How a young designer got brought on to help redesign a legacy sports brand following a TikTok post.

Detectives desperate to locate a 12-year-old, seen abused online, found a surprising lead.

Clips of Deadpool and other film characters have sparked alarm within Hollywood over copyright infringement.

Bournemouth University has surveyed adults in 35 countries on their use of artificial intelligence.

The UK Atomic Energy Authority developed the robot with the European nuclear research centre, Cern.

The milestone procedure went well, with patient Paul Buxton saying he felt "fantastic".

TfL insists it has "kept customers informed throughout this incident and will continue to take all necessary action".

Dr Gilly Carr says it must be clear so people know what is fake and what is historical evidence.

Darren Grimes says his critics should focus on County Durham's problems rather than his use of AI.

A major online food retailer has started trialling self-driving robots to deliver meals in Sunderland.

Politicians say economic benefits of data centres must be balanced with environmental concerns.

Divine Jacob feels AI creates extra barriers for job hunters but analysts say the use of AI is an "evolving picture".

The case is the first wrongful death case against Google over alleged harms caused by Gemini.

Videos, including of glasses-wearers using the toilet or having sex, are sometimes reviewed by a Kenya-based subcontractor.

A Gloucestershire farmer says he uses artificial intelligence to monitor the behaviour of his cows.

The region has been positioning itself as an AI hub, following billions of dollars in investment by tech giants.

AI is being used to screen care workers – but can a robot truly judge who is right for the job?

Weymouth tattoo parlour owner says AI can save time but it can also make the job harder.
